messages.text: hey did anyone update the EB config after the python version bump? i'm seeing some weird behavior in staging
yeah i pushed a change to the Procfile last Thursday, should be reflected. what are you seeing?
nvm seems fine now, probably a cache thing
heads up — i'm adding a `promotions_config` table in the next migration. will post the schema here before i push
👍 make sure there's an index on `active` since the Celery job will be querying it pretty frequently
are we still doing Redis 6 or did we move to 7? i want to make sure the local docker-compose matches staging
staging is on 6.2 still. i was going to upgrade after BRW-38 shipped but never got around to it
i've been running 7 locally for the past month lol, hasn't caused any issues yet
that should be fine for local but let's not let that drift go too long
<@U_DANIELCHO> <@U_MARCUSWEBB> the BRW-44 Celery job is basically done. multiplier logic is in, reading from `promotions_config`. i have it capped at 5x right now, is that right?
no idea, that's a product question. <@U_PRIYANAIR> do you know what the cap is supposed to be?
i think it was 3x but let me double check the notes from that product call
yeah 3x, confirmed with Sara. updating the ticket now
ok will fix, thx
is the staging firebase project the same one we use for prod or is it a separate app?
separate project. `brewgo-staging` in the Firebase console. prod is `brewgo-prod`. credentials are in 1Password under "Firebase Staging"

ok because i think the push notification issue (BRW-45) might actually be a token registration thing. i'm seeing the right device tokens in our db but firebase might have invalidated some of them and we're not handling that response code
oh that would make sense. firebase returns a 200 even when the token is stale in some cases, you have to check the response body
exactly, there's a `results` array in the legacy HTTP response and if `error: NotRegistered` is in there we're supposed to purge the token. we're definitely not doing that right now
ok so we might have a backlog of bad tokens in the db. let's not delete them yet, maybe just flag them so we can audit first
agreed, i'll add a `token_valid` boolean to `device_tokens` and flip it to false when we get that error
sounds good. i'll write up what we know in the ticket
<@U_DANIELCHO> one more thing on BRW-45 — are we using the legacy FCM API or the v1 API? the token error handling is different between them
legacy i'm pretty sure. the code in `notifications.py` uses `https://fcm.googleapis.com/fcm/send`
yeah that's the legacy endpoint. google is deprecating that in June so we'll need to migrate to the v1 API regardless. might be worth doing it as part of this fix rather than coming back to it
+1 on that, let's roll it in. the v1 API uses oauth2 tokens instead of the server key which is a bit more setup but it's not bad
ok i'll look at what that migration involves and report back. might end up being a separate ticket though if it's a big lift
that's fine, let's at least get the token cleanup fix in BRW-45 and we can open a follow-on for the API migration
quick note — i changed the bottom tab navigator to use `@react-navigation/bottom-tabs` v6 instead of v5. had to update some prop names. nothing breaking but heads up if you pull and things look weird
noted, will pull
does anyone remember why we have two separate cart contexts? there's `CartContext` and `ActiveOrderContext` and they seem to overlap a lot

i think `ActiveOrderContext` was added when we did the order tracking screen, it holds the in-flight order state after checkout. `CartContext` is pre-checkout. they're not supposed to overlap but i think they drifted
yeah the `items` array exists in both and they're not always in sync. i'm going to leave it for now but we should clean this up at some point
<@U_DANIELCHO> the rewards points display on the home screen — is that pulling from the local state or making a fresh API call on mount?
should be a fresh call, there's a `useEffect` in `HomeScreen.js` that hits `/api/v1/users/me` on mount
ok because i'm seeing it sometimes show stale points after an order completes. might be a timing thing with how fast the Celery job runs
yeah the job doesn't run instantly, there's a few seconds of lag. we could either poll or just show a "points pending" state until the next fresh load
"points pending" sounds better than polling. i'll add a flag to the order confirmation response so the frontend knows to show it
i can add `points_pending: true` to the order response payload, easy enough
heads up — seeing elevated 500s on `/api/v1/orders` starting around 8am. looking into it now
found it. Redis connection pool was exhausted. the Celery workers were not releasing connections properly after the migration last week. restarting workers now
resolved. 500 rate back to baseline. will write up a short post-mortem
nice catch. should we add connection pool monitoring to the dashboards?
yes, adding that to my list. we should have had an alert on that already

for the post-mortem — the root cause was that i added a Redis call inside the Celery task without using the existing connection pool helper. my bad, i'll make sure future tasks go through `get_redis_connection()`
appreciate the callout, let's add a note to the backend contributing doc so it's clear for the future
got a note forwarded from Sara — a customer is asking whether they can schedule orders in advance, like place an order the night before for 8am pickup. apparently a few people have asked for this
that's been on the backlog for a while. would need some work on the order model to support a `scheduled_for` timestamp and the kitchen display would need updating too
also need to think about what happens if the store is closed at the scheduled time, or if items get 86'd overnight
yeah it's not a small feature. i'll make sure Sara captures it properly so we can estimate it

also this ties into the push notification bug — a customer actually said they waited at the store for 10 minutes because they never got the "ready" notification. that's a real experience issue, BRW-45 should stay high priority
agreed, that one is embarrassing. let's make sure we get it fixed before the weekend rush
